_setup_notes.post_import_step: After importing: 1) Open the 'Execute: Core Workflow' node, click the workflow selector, and choose 'Speed to Lead — Core' by name. This updates the CORE_WORKFLOW_ID placeholder to the actual workflow ID assigned by this n8n instance. 2) Set required env vars: MARTIN_GOOGLE_KEY, MARTIN_TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID, MARTIN_TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN, MARTIN_TWILIO_SENDER_ID. 3) Optional WhatsApp env vars (required only when enabling WhatsApp for this client after WABA onboarding): MARTIN_WHATSAPP_SENDER (E.164 WhatsApp-enabled number), MARTIN_WHATSAPP_TEMPLATE_SID (prospect template SID), MARTIN_WHATSAPP_OWNER_TEMPLATE_SID (owner notification template SID). Then set whatsapp_enabled: true and/or owner_whatsapp_enabled: true in the jsCode. 4) Activate the workflow. 5) Configure Google Ads Lead Form webhook URL to: https://{your-n8n-host}/webhook/cabinet-martin
_setup_notes.google_key_note: Per-client google_key validation happens here in the entry workflow — the Core Workflow trusts the payload it receives from authenticated entry workflows.
_setup_notes.fire_and_forget: waitForSubWorkflow: false is REQUIRED. The Core Workflow contains a Wait node (120 min by default for Cabinet Martin). If waitForSubWorkflow were true, this entry workflow would block for 120+ minutes before returning HTTP 200 to Google Ads — causing timeout and lead loss.
